title: Chrono's Echoes
logline: In a desperate bid to save humanity, a rogue time traveler seeks to reverse his own fate by altering the course of history.
seed_prompt: A time-locked space station receives a distress message that originated from fifty years in the future

characters.name: Ethan
characters.voice_style: His voice is calm and analytical.

world_rules: Time travel can alter historical events but also has consequences on the present and future.

acts.summary: The protagonist discovers an ancient time-locked space station and is drawn into a complex web of secrets and conspiracies.
As the protagonist uncovers evidence of a sinister organization manipulating time travel, he must navigate through a series of escalating confrontations and puzzles.
The protagonist faces a critical decision: either stop the time-travelers' plans for global domination or join them as their ultimate weapon against the universe's timeline.

chapters.title: No code names used in the text itself; use real na
chapters.content:
No code names used in the text itself; use real names instead.
The first sentence ends with "Ethan." Use past tense throughout the entire paragraph. Start your story here:

Ethan opened his eyes slowly, the sterile white walls of the room starkly contrasted with his dark hair and glasses. He sat up straight, taking a deep breath before standing up. There was something different about this place - it felt old, like someone had been living there for centuries. A sense of dread crept over him as he looked around, feeling that same unease from his childhood home again. 

He turned towards the window, where sunlight streamed across the floor, casting long shadows on the ground below. The glass shattered upon impact when they landed nearby. Panic set in, but quickly dissolved into determination. With a quick glance at his watch, Ethan realized he still had some time left until the meeting scheduled with Dr. Sarah Cooper – one of the few people who could help unravel the mystery surrounding the mysterious time machine, or what remained of it.  
Ethan walked briskly down the hall toward the elevator, ignoring the odd glances and whispers of curiosity and suspicion behind him. If anyone saw him now, if anyone even thought to follow him, he would have to fight hard just to keep moving forward. But then he reached the elevators, stepped inside, and pushed the button for the eighth floor. Once he got off, he took another path down the hallway, passing various rooms labeled 'Operations', 'Maintenance', and so forth. Eventually, after several twists and turns, he found himself in front of a door marked 'Research'. Stepping back, Ethan carefully examined the lock. It appeared made out of solid metal, though it seemed almost too thick to be secure. Just as he hesitated, a small, intricate mechanism within revealed itself beneath the surface—a hidden lever. Using the lever, Ethan twisted the lock until it clicked open, revealing the keyhole above. Inserting the spare key he carried, the door swung wide once more. Inside were shelves full of books, maps, diagrams, and other documentation related to time travel. Many of these documents dated back hundreds of years, suggesting the existence of a powerful group controlling such knowledge—or perhaps the opposite. Whatever it contained, whatever secret lay locked away behind those doors...or maybe not. That didn't seem important right now. What mattered most was getting answers from whoever stood guard outside—Dr. Cooper. Ethan moved swiftly along the rows of books, examining every document, trying to find clues leading back to the original source of the device. But nothing panned out. He couldn’t afford to waste any time looking elsewhere—he needed information. Then came the moment of truth—the revelation. An old photograph hung near the edge of one shelf, its edges faded and cracked, showing details lost under countless hours of exposure. Its title read simply—"Chronos". Curiosity prickling at his mind, Ethan bent down to pick it up. Holding it close to the light, he studied the picture closely. Onlooker? Or was that just imagination running wild? Either way, it was strange. Wasn't it always meant to be? And why did it show two figures together, both holding hands, smiling, laughing, engaged in conversation—yet seemingly oblivious of the camera’s presence? This puzzled him deeply.Even more baffled was how much the scene resembled his mother and father. They had been married just three months ago, less than half a year since she died. Not sure whether he should rush out of the room to inform them, Ethan decided he’d better go check. Reaching for the doorknob, he turned it slightly, hoping it wasn’t locked. It slid smoothly into position. Opening the door, Ethan entered quietly, closing the door behind him immediately afterwards. Still uncertain whether he wanted to alert her or not—and remembering his earlier thoughts concerning the possibility of misreading the photo—he went to sit beside the bed in the corner, pulling up chairs while keeping watch over the area from which she'd come last night. When he arrived, his heart pounded with anxiety and fear. She was lying in bed peacefully, sleeping soundly. Nothing suspicious was happening. So far, things hadn't changed since yesterday evening. Maybe it was best not to disturb her sleep further, especially without knowing exactly what she knew.
